steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence opens with the participial phrase "Having 9,721 feet of prominence," which must be immediately followed by the noun it modifies — the thing that actually possesses that topographical prominence. Any other subject creates a dangling modifier.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Pico do Ramelau, a peak located in Timor-Leste, is ranked by geographers as the world's 98th most prominent mountain.: the mountain itself has the prominence; the modifier correctly attaches to it. ✓
  • Choice B — the list of mountains geographers rank as the world's most prominent includes Pico do Ramelau, a peak located in Timor-Leste, at number 98.: a list cannot possess topographical prominence. Dangling modifier. ✗
  • Choice C — geographers have ranked Pico do Ramelau, a peak in Timor-Leste, as the world's 98th most prominent mountain.: people do not have 9,721 feet of prominence. Dangling modifier. ✗
  • Choice D — the ranking given by geographers to Pico do Ramelau, a peak located in Timor-Leste, is 98th most prominent mountain in the world.: an abstract ranking cannot possess physical prominence. Dangling modifier. ✗
A is correct.
